signals.reasoning: BTC is liquid and relatively stable, but the directional edge is still muted. Slightly negative funding offers some support, yet the board remains low-signal and macro is still RED, so there is not enough asymmetry to justify a fresh long or short.

ETH is still one of the weaker majors with a negative 24h trend and a softer structure than BTC. This is not a high-conviction breakdown short, but it still leans better as a fade than as a dip-buy while macro risk stays elevated.
SOL is weak but not decisively broken, and the board still marks it no-trade. Negative funding reduces short appeal, while the downside impulse is not strong enough to justify a clean bearish swing.

TNSR still has one of the strongest squeeze-continuation profiles on the board: explosive 24h momentum, extremely negative funding, and a top-tier squeeze score. Confidence is capped only because the name is still wide and macro remains RED, so this is a reduced-size momentum buy rather than a relaxed swing.

KAITO is deeply down on the day, but the short thesis weakened because funding flipped slightly negative instead of staying crowded-long positive. That removes some downside asymmetry, so I would avoid forcing a fresh trade here.

DYM is one of the better long candidates in this batch. It combines positive price expansion with strongly negative funding and elite squeeze/overcrowding scores, which is the classic continuation setup. The main risk is execution quality because it is still a small and wide market.

ZEREBRO has positive 24h momentum, but the board is mixed and funding is mildly positive rather than supportive of a squeeze. It is moving, but not with a clean enough edge to outrank the stronger continuation names.
CHILLGUY is up hard, but the spread is extremely poor and the setup is too unstable to trust as a clean continuation entry. Positive funding also means you are not being paid by a squeeze setup here.

APEX is one of the cleaner bearish names on this sheet: negative 24h trend, positive funding, and a mechanical bearish lean. It is not as liquid as the majors, but the setup is cleaner than trying to short the stronger squeeze names.

BLUR remains the best long on the board. It still has strong upside expansion, deeply negative funding, a high squeeze score, and materially better volume quality than most of the other small caps. Macro conditions still haircut confidence, but directionally this is the best continuation candidate here.

GRIFFAIN has positive momentum and decent liquidity, but the signal is mixed because funding is still positive and the board does not give a strong directional bias. It is interesting, but not clean enough to rank ahead of BLUR, DYM, or TNSR.
